attributes.action: Introduce a Citizen-Initiated Referendum to Trigger a General Election.
attributes.additional_details:
Parliament should pass a law allowing citizens to trigger a General Election via a secure online vote of no confidence.
Governments can lose support mid-term but serve up to five years. This would strengthen democracy with a high bar: 75% No Confidence and 60% turnout to automatically dissolve Parliament and call a GE within 6 weeks.
This balanced reform would restore trust and improve accountability.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
attributes.background: Parliament should allow citizens to trigger a General Election through a secure online vote of no confidence. It would pass only with 75% voting No Confidence and 60% turnout, automatically dissolving Parliament.

rejection.details:
We don't know when this referendum should take place or how its timing would be triggered. You may want to sign this similar one:  

https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/758722
